creator: citrin@citrin.ru
text:
pkg_info -g allow to check package integrity, but exit code always 0
even if checksum for some files mismatch.

This makes pkg_info -g hard to use in shell scripts.

Fix: exit with non zero exit code, when checksum mismatch discovered.

Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: 1. install some package
2. edit some of installed files
3. run:
pkg_info -g package
echo $?

pkg_info -g will show checksum mismatch, but exit code will be 0.
